bug_description: The starting row of each antidiagonal is computed as `max(0, d - cols)` instead of the correct `max(0, d - cols + 1)`. For diagonals that span beyond the rightmost column, this shifts `r_start` one row too low, causing the first element of those diagonals to reference an out-of-bounds column index `c = d - r` that is negative (or incorrect). The bug affects all antidiagonals with index `d >= cols`, i.e., all diagonals in the lower-left portion of non-square and square matrices wider than one column.
